description: Produced a campaign for the Omnicom Health of Advertising Challenge
body_my_role: Project Manager leading the team across research, concept development, strategic direction, and final pitch execution.
body_insight: People recognize osteoporosis as a serious condition, but many younger adults do not see themselves as personally at risk.
body_problem: Because osteoporosis is often associated with older age, younger audiences tend to dismiss it as distant or irrelevant, limiting early awareness and family-level concern.
body_solution: We created 'OOF' a cross-generational campaign platform built around a term that functions both as a younger meme reference and a familiar expression of physical discomfort, then extended it across illustrated social ads, billboard and OOH concepts, and OOF.org as a clear destination for bone-health education and action.
image_sequence_count: 8 (hero excluded from sequence — listed in imageOrder but filtered out)
notes: Only project with image sequence (8 images). 3 highlighted full-bleed. No scope field. Body uses bold-label Markdown (**My role:** etc). description ends without period.

page_title: Fordham University Spring Weekend Lineup Reveal
description: A social-first concert lineup reveal that turned student survey input into a high-anticipation campaign moment for Fordham University Spring Weekend.
body_my_role: Creative lead shaping the rollout strategy, editing approach, and final reveal asset for Spring Weekend.
body_insight: Students care as much about how the lineup is revealed as the lineup itself, especially when the announcement is one of the few visible proofs that their input mattered.
body_problem: Traditional PR-style lineup announcements felt static and disconnected from student culture, making a high-anticipation moment feel less exciting and less reflective of campus engagement.
body_solution: I transformed the reveal into a social-first video built from survey data, sharper pacing, and a more cohesive visual theme, creating CAB's most liked lineup reveal and helping support the most successful Spring Weekend of my four years at Fordham, with 72% of 926 surveyed students rating the events a 5/5, over 90% rating them a 4 or higher, and an overall average of 4.6.
notes: Only project with LinkedIn embed. Role is 'President + Editor' — different convention from others (others say 'Project Manager'). tags include 'project' which is unusual. No scope field. No external URL.

description: Designed The Residency, a 30-day Airbnb belonging system that helps newcomers turn a move into real social and cultural entry through hosts, cohorts, and neighborhood access.
body_my_role: Project Manager driving strategic alignment, team direction, and pitch development from insight to final presentation.
body_insight: You can live in a city for years and still feel like a tourist.
body_problem: For Gen Z relocators, moving often solves for access but not connection, leaving people lonely, socially invisible, and disconnected from the real culture around them.
body_solution: We designed The Residency, a 30-day Airbnb program that helps newcomers build belonging through a Residency Host, cohort-based connection, and tools like the Neighborhood Key, reframing Airbnb from a place to stay into a system for social and cultural entry.
body_extra_text: The full project lives on the dedicated website: [View Full Project Website](https://infja.netlify.app/)
notes: Has externalUrl — shows CTA button + URL below narrative. Extra body line references the external site. description has a trailing newline. date is 2026 (future — intentional?).

description: A speculative Uber Eats activation that used sidewalk delivery robots to outmaneuver traffic, crowd congestion, and sponsorship restrictions during Philadelphia's World Cup and July 4th chaos.
body_my_role: Project Manager leading team coordination, concept shaping, narrative structure, and final pitch development.
body_insight: Fans plan obsessively for the match itself, but not for the moment they run out of what they need and realize they cannot leave to get it.
body_problem: In a high-density event like the World Cup in Philadelphia, road closures, crowd congestion, and sponsor-controlled zones - how can we remind World Cup fan that Uber can deliver almost almost anything.
body_solution: We created The Robot Bypass, a real-time ambush concept that positioned Uber Eats Avride robot fleet as the only delivery system built for citywide gridlock, using sidewalk mobility, strategic deployment zones, and a culturally tuned robot persona to let the brand reach stranded fans where cars, couriers, and even competitors could not.
body_extra_text: The full project lives on the dedicated website: [View Full Project Website](https://uberbrief.netlify.app/)
notes: 'Philidelphia' is misspelled in the title (should be Philadelphia) — appears on the home card and detail page h1. Problem field has 'almost almost' (duplicate word). Has externalUrl. date is 2026 (future — same pattern as airbnb).
